page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores using the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations position the m6gd.8xlarge in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score is 48563.19, and the PassMark CPU score is 17388.36. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, with a Geekbench single-core score of 950.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 20.31 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 1894.51 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 166.08 GB/sec for large-block reads. Database operations perform steadily, as evidenced by a PassMark database score of 20452.11 and Redis SET operations achieving 36.57 million operations per second.

The inclusion of 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside 32 dedicated ARM cores offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring fast local scratch space without the added cost of external block storage. Given its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and solid multi-threaded performance, the m6gd.8xlarge is suited for application servers, multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. It is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM text generation, where it exhibits lower performance.
description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the ARM64 architecture, featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers balanced resource allocation with 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Benchmark data shows average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48563.19 and a PassMark database score of 20452.11, though single-core metrics are weaker. The bundled local NVMe storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive applications. This instance is well-suited for multi-threaded databases, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose containerized workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring thirty-two dedicated cores, balanced memory, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for multi-threaded workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
